name: Guarantee Views Or Revenue
current_truth: LWA must never guarantee views, revenue, payouts, or virality.
missing_piece: No missing implementation. This is a permanent claim boundary.
next_step: Keep sales, SEO, App Store, and Whop copy inside safe language.
user_facing_copy: LWA helps prepare stronger clip packages. It does not guarantee results.
internal_notes: Treat this as a brand and compliance rule, not a backlog item.

name: Replace Human Editor
current_truth: LWA accelerates clip selection, packaging, and review, but it does not replace a human editor end-to-end yet.
missing_piece: Deeper local editing, caption burn-in profiles, reusable templates, and richer timeline control.
next_step: Improve local editing and export options without pretending to be a full editing suite.
user_facing_copy: LWA helps your editor move faster with ranked clip packages and packaging guidance.
internal_notes: Keep positioning closer to accelerator than replacement.

name: Direct Social Posting
current_truth: LWA prepares clips for platforms but does not direct-post yet.
missing_piece: Platform auth, posting queues, provider compliance, and account connection management.
next_step: Add posting integrations only after auth and compliance are stable.
user_facing_copy: LWA helps you prepare clips for posting. Publishing stays manual for now.
internal_notes: Do not imply direct TikTok, Instagram, or YouTube upload until real integrations exist.

name: Whop Campaign Browsing
current_truth: Whop workflow direction exists, but native campaign browsing needs Whop API integration.
missing_piece: Server-side Whop verification, campaign import, and access mapping.
next_step: Build Whop verification and campaign import foundation.
user_facing_copy: Built for Whop-style clipping workflows. Native Whop campaign browsing is future work.
internal_notes: Keep customer copy careful until verified server-side.

name: Campaign Submission Automation
current_truth: LWA can help prepare campaign-ready packages, but automatic submission is future work.
missing_piece: Campaign import, submission APIs, audit trails, and operator review controls.
next_step: Build submission flow only after Whop foundation and review states are stable.
user_facing_copy: LWA can help package campaign-ready clips. Submission stays manual today.
internal_notes: Do not sell payout automation or submission automation yet.

name: Private Or Login-Gated Video Processing
current_truth: LWA should not bypass login-gated or private platforms. Authorized upload and import flows are the future-safe path.
missing_piece: Authorized imports, user-owned uploads, and compliant source connectors.
next_step: Expand upload-first and authorized import workflows rather than bypassing source restrictions.
user_facing_copy: Use public sources or your own uploaded media. Private platform bypass is not supported.
internal_notes: This is a compliance boundary as much as a roadmap item.

name: AI Provider Reliability
current_truth: AI providers can fail or run out of credits. LWA has fallback behavior and local packaging paths.
missing_piece: Provider health alerts, key monitoring, richer fallback quality, and clearer operator telemetry.
next_step: Keep improving provider health checks and fallback quality.
user_facing_copy: LWA is AI-assisted and fallback-aware. Results stay useful when providers are limited.
internal_notes: Do not make provider success a hard requirement for usable output.

name: Account Workspace Team System
current_truth: A full account, workspace, and team model is not implemented yet.
missing_piece: Durable auth, workspace membership, team roles, and shared state beyond local/mobile history.
next_step: Add auth and workspace model after launch-critical clipping flows are stable.
user_facing_copy: LWA is still in a single-user or lightweight-account phase.
internal_notes: Do not imply agency workspace support until durable storage and permissions exist.

name: Generated Asset Retention
current_truth: Generated assets now have a retention path, but Railway storage behavior still needs production verification.
missing_piece: Deployment validation, storage monitoring, and scheduled cleanup if traffic grows.
next_step: Verify asset_retention.py, startup cleanup, protected-path safety, and Railway volume behavior.
user_facing_copy: Generated assets are retained for a limited time and may be cleaned up automatically.
internal_notes: Backend volume warning should stay in the launch checklist until Railway confirms cleanup is healthy.

name: Live Twitch Ingestion
current_truth: Twitch intelligence foundations can exist without live ingestion. Real live ingest is future work.
missing_piece: EventSub, chat collectors, recording ingest, and stream-state workers.
next_step: Add Twitch ingestion only after worker architecture and webhook verification are ready.
user_facing_copy: Built with Twitch intelligence in mind. Live ingest is still future work.
internal_notes: Avoid claiming official live Twitch integration.

name: Caption Burn-In Profiles
current_truth: Caption artifacts may exist, but full styled burn-in profile support is not finished yet.
missing_piece: FFmpeg caption style presets, export profile selection, and render worker separation.
next_step: Build a caption profile engine after retention and core rendering stability are verified.
user_facing_copy: Caption packaging is available. Advanced burn-in styles are still evolving.
internal_notes: Keep caption artifacts separate from styled burn-in claims.

name: Analytics Performance Feedback
current_truth: An event-log foundation exists, but a full analytics dashboard and performance feedback loop are still future work.
missing_piece: Internal summary views, posted-URL feedback, and user-facing performance analysis.
next_step: Start with JSONL event logs, then build internal summaries before public dashboards.
user_facing_copy: LWA is building a stronger feedback loop around what gets reviewed, opened, and shared.
internal_notes: Keep analytics small, structured, nonfatal, and privacy-conscious.

name: Upload-First Clipping
current_truth: Upload-first and local-fallback clipping is a core live direction.
missing_piece: Broader source support and more polished ingest UX, not a missing core capability.
next_step: Keep improving upload handling and source-type support while preserving fallback behavior.
user_facing_copy: Upload-first clipping is live and remains the primary workflow.
internal_notes: This should stay central even as URL and campaign flows grow.

name: Rendered Vs Strategy Packages
current_truth: LWA separates playable rendered clips from strategy-only packages.
missing_piece: Consistency polish across every client surface, not missing core behavior.
next_step: Keep artifact handling, fallback copy, and preview truth consistent across clients.
user_facing_copy: Rendered clips and strategy packages are separated so the workflow stays honest.
internal_notes: This is a differentiator and should stay explicit in product surfaces.

name: Quota Entitlements
current_truth: Server-side quota tracking and API-key plan unlocks exist, but abuse hardening and Whop verification are still in progress.
missing_piece: Production validation, richer error handling, Whop verification, and longer-term persistence.
next_step: Harden quota abuse, verify retention behavior, then add Whop entitlement verification.
user_facing_copy: Usage limits and paid-access foundations exist, but higher-volume access is still evolving.
internal_notes: Keep public copy honest: API-key unlocks are live, Whop verification is not.
